typecheck_backend.details: 95 errors in 13 files — pre-existing type errors scheduled for fix in milestone 'deployment' (feature deploy-mypy-fixes). Not introduced by video-replay.

lint_frontend.details: 1 error: 'VideoEntry' is defined but never used in ReportPage.tsx:10. 10 warnings (complexity/max-lines — pre-existing). The unused import was introduced by fix-video-parsing-and-tests when VideoEntry type was moved to video.ts but the import was kept in ReportPage.tsx.

validatorNote: Backend mypy fails with 95 pre-existing type errors not introduced by video-replay (scheduled for deployment milestone). Frontend ESLint has 1 new error: unused VideoEntry import in ReportPage.tsx — introduced by fix-video-parsing-and-tests when parsing was extracted to shared video.ts module. This is a trivial fix (remove the unused import) but does not block functionality. 10 ESLint warnings are pre-existing complexity/max-lines issues. All tests pass (275 backend + 158 frontend). TypeScript strict check passes.

nonBlockingIssues.description: ReportPage.tsx line 10 imports 'VideoEntry' type from '../lib/video' but never uses it directly. This causes an ESLint error (@typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars). Fix: remove 'type VideoEntry' from the import statement.
nova_act_browser.py line 317: `video_urls if video_urls else {}` is redundant since video_urls is already initialized as {} and empty dict is falsy. Could simply be `video_urls=video_urls`.

video.test.ts line 53: Test 'prefers longer scenario match over shorter partial match' has misleading name — the function iterates in array order, not by length. With real taxonomy data this is harmless but the test name overpromises.

PersonaDiffPage.test.tsx line 590: Video test waits on `getByText(/2 differences found/i)` which couples the video test to differences calculation. A more resilient wait target would be persona names or video link text.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Reinforce that manual browser verification (Step 6) is MANDATORY even when all automated tests pass. The fix-video-parsing-and-tests worker skipped Step 6 despite the prior review specifically identifying that automated tests can mask UI bugs. Add: 'If a feature is a FIX for a review failure that cited test reliability, manual verification is especially critical — do not skip it.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: fix-video-parsing-and-tests handoff has empty interactiveChecks array. The prior review of video-frontend-player specifically flagged that getAllByText matched hero pills instead of video labels, making manual verification crucial for this fix. This is the second consecutive worker on this feature to skip manual verification.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: 'When extracting code to shared utilities, ensure the old import site only imports what it still uses. Run ESLint after refactoring to catch unused imports.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: fix-video-parsing-and-tests moved VideoEntry type to video.ts but left the import in ReportPage.tsx, causing an ESLint error. This is a common refactoring oversight.

rejectedObservations.observation: Thread-safe data propagation pattern (shared dict + lock through 3 call layers) should be documented in AGENTS.md or library
rejectedObservations.reason: The pattern is self-evident in the code after the fix. Documenting it in AGENTS.md would be overly specific to one provider. If it becomes a repeated pattern across providers, it could be documented then.

rejectedObservations.observation: Handoff commitId discrepancy in fix-nova-act-video-url-collection (hash vs actual commit)
rejectedObservations.reason: Same issue noted in round 1. Metadata recording error, not actionable through code changes.

rejectedObservations.observation: Library entry video-data-model.md effectively guided the fix worker — validates library approach
rejectedObservations.reason: Positive observation, not an actionable change. The library approach is already established.
